summary: Statement-level SSOT consolidation for repeated governance hard-gate, context-routing, docs-routing, and prompt-scaffold wording. The ledger records the exact statement owner, downstream duplicate families, allowed downstream form, and necessity test result before applying demotion edits.
verification_commands: rg -n "Hard gates|Hard gate:|Context injection \(REQUIRED\)|fallback_inject|default_inject|docs cannot become|routing-only|router-only|Read and follow `?AGENTS\.md" README.md docs/agents docs/project templates --glob '*.md'
invariants: INV-D-1: A governance statement family has one highest governing owner; downstream docs may keep only citations, witnesses, scaffolds, examples, or declared local authority facts.
INV-D-2: Context-routing facts remain owned by agents-manifest.yaml; downstream docs record manifest-resolution witnesses without copying routing mechanics.
INV-D-3: Copy/paste prompts and loader stubs may keep short executable safety scaffolds only when self-contained execution requires them.
INV-OBS-1: Governance checks must not require downstream docs to keep duplicate hard-gate wording in sync with a local canonical section.

witnesses.signal: Statement ledger groups repeated statement families by owner and records downstream actions.
witnesses.pass_criteria: Each statement family lists owner_path, duplicate locations, action, replacement rule, and necessity result.

witnesses.signal: Downstream governance-learning and context-retrieval text refers to current manifest routing instead of maintaining a local injected-doc or fallback list.
witnesses.pass_criteria: No downstream prompt/playbook text owns fallback/default/profile routing facts outside agents-manifest.yaml.

witnesses.signal: Copy/paste prompt blocks explicitly label retained hard-gate lines as scaffold sourced from AGENTS.md.
witnesses.pass_criteria: Retained repeated safety lines are minimal and do not include manifest internals or full policy prose.

witnesses.signal: Governance checker requires authority references and prompt scaffold markers instead of exact hard-gate parity.
witnesses.pass_criteria: Tests cover the authority-reference structure and do not require a duplicate canonical hard-gates section.

statement_ledger.statement_family: Read and follow AGENTS.md before work
statement_ledger.replacement_wording_rule: Narrative docs cite AGENTS.md. Copy/paste prompt and loader blocks may keep one short executable line labeled as scaffold sourced from AGENTS.md.
statement_ledger.necessity_result: keep only in copy/paste scaffolds because the text may be used outside the repo context

statement_ledger.statement_family: Execute context injection and load routed docs
statement_ledger.owner_path: AGENTS.md plus routing facts in agents-manifest.yaml
statement_ledger.replacement_wording_rule: Use 'execute the current AGENTS.md Context Injection Procedure using agents-manifest.yaml' and record the witness; do not name fallback/default/profile lists outside the manifest.
statement_ledger.necessity_result: context-retrieval keeps witness fields; playbooks and README keep only pointers

statement_ledger.statement_family: Docs cannot become duplicate or non-owner SSOT records
statement_ledger.replacement_wording_rule: Downstream docs cite docs SSOT policy and keep only local scaffold fields or local project-rule facts.
statement_ledger.necessity_result: keep local examples and fields only where the document is a template or project-local owner

statement_ledger.statement_family: Router files are routing-only and follow registry-resolved names
statement_ledger.replacement_wording_rule: Templates may show scaffold file names; policy language routes to docs SSOT policy and the registry.
statement_ledger.necessity_result: project-docs-template keeps concrete scaffold outputs; non-template docs cite owners

statement_ledger.statement_family: Project docs branch placement and data-truth ownership
statement_ledger.replacement_wording_rule: The docs policy owns placement rules; project-docs-template owns copy/paste scaffold shape; README routes to project docs.
statement_ledger.necessity_result: template examples remain because they are copy/paste scaffolds

allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.allowed_pattern: Open and follow .governance/AGENTS.md, execute its current Context Injection Procedure, and keep project docs under docs/project.
allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.forbidden_pattern: Listing fallback_inject, default_inject, profile mechanics, or full governance policy.

allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.allowed_pattern: Owner citation plus local procedure/scaffold fields.
allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.forbidden_pattern: Hard gates (canonical; keep wording in sync) or local copies of manifest routing lists.

allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.allowed_pattern: Short 'copy/paste scaffold sourced from AGENTS.md' lines needed for execution outside repo context.
allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.forbidden_pattern: Long policy lists or manifest internals.

allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.allowed_pattern: Title plus direct-child route bullets with Required when text.
allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.forbidden_pattern: Child policy summaries or copied hard gates.

allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.allowed_pattern: Declared local facts, owners, witnesses, and project-specific constraints.
allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.forbidden_pattern: Copies of global governance rules or non-owner constants/defaults/data.

allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.allowed_pattern: Validate presence of owner citations, scaffold labels, and forbidden duplicate headings.
allowed_downstream_wording_patterns.forbidden_pattern: Enforce exact repeated hard-gate wording across downstream docs.

reviewers.scope: Owner hierarchy, README stubs, playbook duplication, manifest leakage
Timed out before final usable evidence
Copy/paste prompt exceptions, template scaffold ownership, context witness language
README checks, Python version, dirty worktree, checker enforcement

findings.issue: Loader examples repeated manifest and context-injection mechanics.
findings.recommendation: Keep only minimal loader scaffold and remove fallback/profile mechanics.

findings.issue: A local canonical hard-gates section preserved duplicated upstream rules.
findings.recommendation: Replace with Authority References and a short copy/paste prompt scaffold.

findings.issue: Template frontmatter and hard-gate heading blurred policy ownership with scaffold ownership.
findings.recommendation: Make the template self-owned for scaffold shape and cite AGENTS/docs policy for governing rules.

findings.issue: Manifest field names can look like local routing policy.

council_summary.conflicts: Council returned HOLD for blind consolidation but GO for a constrained patch that keeps minimal copy/paste scaffolds and local project-authority records.
council_summary.reconciliation_decision: accepted constrained patch; rejected blind deletion and over-promotion into AGENTS.md
council_summary.residual_risks: One silent-error reviewer timed out; semantic duplication still needs broader future checker coverage beyond this first stable duplicate class.

findings_fixed: README loader stubs now use one shared body plus per-tool title list.
governance-learnings-template.md now cites docs policy/project-docs-template instead of carrying a project-doc placement matrix.
scripts/check_governance_core/_repo_and_governance.py now validates required scaffold gates inside the extracted prompt pack, with regression coverage.
post_change_scan.residual_risk: Semantic duplicate detection still relies on the statement ledger and grep witness for broader statement families; current checker coverage targets the first stable duplicate class only.

validation_context.evidence_plan: Use rg statement-family searches as semantic witnesses, then run README-declared structural checks with Python 3.12 where Python 3.11+ is required.
